seoTitle: How to Use Button Slicer in Power BI Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to add and format the Button Slicer visual in Power BI, including grid layout, hover states, and selected-state styling.
intro: Standard checkbox or dropdown slicers can feel clunky and visually inconsistent on a polished dashboard. This guide shows how to use Power BI's Button Slicer visual to create a responsive grid of filter buttons with custom hover and selected states. You'll learn how to configure rows and columns, style each button state, and even link external icon images to your filter options.

steps.heading: Add the Button Slicer visual
steps.body: In the Visualizations pane, find and select the Button Slicer icon, then drag your target field into the Field bucket.

steps.heading: Configure the grid layout
steps.body: In the Format pane, expand Layout and choose horizontal or vertical alignment, then adjust the Rows and Columns counts to shape your button grid.

steps.heading: Style the hover state
steps.body: Expand the Button card, select the State dropdown, choose Hover, and set a background fill or shadow effect for visual feedback.

steps.heading: Style the selected state
steps.body: Switch the State dropdown to Selected and assign a distinct accent color so users can clearly see active filters.

steps.heading: Add text formatting or images
steps.body: Adjust typography and multi-line text in the Callout Values section, or link external icon URLs through the Images container for a more visual layout.

faq.question: How is Button Slicer different from a regular slicer?
faq.answer: Button Slicer lets you build a customizable grid with distinct hover and selected states, plus optional images, instead of a plain checkbox or dropdown list.

faq.question: Can I add icons to my button slicer options?
faq.answer: Yes, the Images container in the formatting panel lets you link external icon URLs directly to individual slicer buttons.

faq.question: How do I arrange buttons into multiple rows?
faq.answer: Adjust the Rows and Columns values in the Layout section of the Format pane to control how your buttons are arranged.

faq.question: Does the button slicer filter the whole report page?
faq.answer: By default it filters visuals on the same page like any standard slicer, and you can sync it across pages using Power BI's slicer sync feature.
